title: pilostaxis rugelii
intro: Pilostaxis rugelii, also known as Rugel's pilostaxis or wild lettuce, is a perennial herb belonging to the dandelion family, native to North America. The plant's leaves, roots, and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, headaches, and inflammation. The leaves and roots of Pilostaxis rugelii are rich in sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, which possess analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, offering health benefits such as pain relief, reduced inflammation, and improved digestion. Pilostaxis rugelii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal supplements and topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
